name: Lemon Squeezy API
what_it_does: Lemon Squeezy's API for selling digital products and subscriptions as a merchant of record, with built-in tax compliance, licensing, and affiliate management for indie developers.
use_cases: Selling digital products and software licenses via agents
Managing subscription plans and customer billing automation
Issuing and validating software license keys
Generating discount codes and managing promotions
Retrieving sales and revenue analytics for reporting agents
not_for: Physical goods or high-volume enterprise commerce
Complex custom checkout flows requiring full UI control
Teams needing direct Stripe/payment processor integration
High-volume businesses where 5% fees are prohibitive
best_when: An indie developer or small SaaS business needs merchant of record services with simple API access for digital product sales and subscription management.
avoid_when: You need enterprise-grade billing features, complex pricing, or direct payment processor control.

auth.notes: Simple API key authentication. Bearer token in Authorization header. Keys generated from dashboard. No scopes — full account access. Easy for agents.

pricing.notes: Revenue share model similar to Paddle. Free to sign up and test. Charges only on sales. Good for low-volume indie businesses; expensive at scale.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Error responses are JSON-API compliant with title and detail fields. Error messages are generally readable but documentation on specific error codes is thin.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No idempotency key support. Agents must implement their own deduplication.
known_agent_gotchas: API follows JSON:API spec — response structure is more complex than standard REST
No idempotency keys — risky for agents retrying failed requests
Webhook signature verification uses X-Signature header with SHA256 HMAC
Rate limits not publicly documented — agents may hit undocumented limits
License key validation requires separate endpoint from general API
Smaller developer ecosystem and SDK support compared to Stripe/Paddle
